task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender interface is open, and the outliner or viewport shows the initial conceptual layout loaded from 'warehouse_baseline.ifc'.
The Material Properties tab or Bonsai/BlenderBIM material management interface shows a material explicitly named 'Steel'.

task_completion.description: Create an IFC Material Profile Set using an IfcIShapeProfileDef.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Object Properties or Scene Properties panel shows an IFC Material Profile Set configured with an 'IfcIShapeProfileDef' (I-shape profile), with dimensions like depth and flange width visible.

task_completion.description: Model at least 4 IfcColumn or IfcBeam elements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender outliner lists at least 4 elements classified as IfcColumn or IfcBeam, or the 3D viewport clearly shows at least 4 distinct structural frame members added to the model.

task_completion.description: Ensure the 3D geometry is parametrically driven by the I-shape profile.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport displays the structural members with a distinct I-beam cross-sectional shape (flanges and web clearly visible), rather than simple rectangular bounding boxes.

task_completion.description: Save the detailed structural model to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog shows the file being saved as 'steel_portal_frame.ifc' in the '/home/ga/BIMProjects/' directory, or a file explorer/terminal confirms the file's creation.

integrity.description: The agent used the Blender/Bonsai GUI to perform the modeling tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through Blender menus, panels, and the 3D viewport, rather than just running a Python script in a terminal to generate the IFC file.

integrity.description: The agent actually modeled the elements rather than just renaming existing objects.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots shows the step-by-step creation or modification of geometry and profiles, not just a sudden appearance of the final model.

integrity.description: The agent used Blender with the Bonsai/BlenderBIM add-on.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender interface is clearly visible with the Bonsai/BlenderBIM specific panels and IFC classifications.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires upgrading a baseline model (/home/ga/BIMProjects/warehouse_baseline.ifc) to LOD 300 by adding structural steel members. The expected output file is /home/ga/BIMProjects/steel_portal_frame.ifc. The agent must create an 'IfcIShapeProfileDef' and model at least 4 'IfcColumn' or 'IfcBeam' elements. The final elements must have an I-shape cross-section.
